title: Jaws Wired Shut

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic character catchphrase that's been culturally significant for decades. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, they're showing the filling now. Can they do that?
jokes.setup: Homer reading what appears to be a dental magazine
jokes.punchline: Treats dental procedures like inappropriate content
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about Homer's naive perspective on mundane things.

jokes.text: What the hell is that? Rainbows. Tank tops. Empowerment.
jokes.setup: Homer confused by gay pride parade
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact listing of pride parade elements
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's confusion and Marge's casual acceptance. Nice economy in the three-item list.

jokes.text: This is the gay pride parade! Woo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Homer was just confused by the parade
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to enthusiastic support
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer flip-flop behavior showing his childlike enthusiasm once he understands something.

jokes.text: We're here! We're queer! Get used to it! / You do this every year! We are used to it! / Spoil sport!
jokes.punchline: Moe pointing out the logical flaw in the chant's premise
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of a familiar chant with logical criticism. Great structure and earned humor.

jokes.text: We're gay. We're glad. But don't tell Mom and Dad.
jokes.punchline: Unexpected admission about still being closeted to parents
jokes.explanation: Nice rhyme scheme with a relatable twist about family dynamics.

jokes.text: Wouldn't it be great if that man and woman got together?
jokes.punchline: Marge completely missing the point of who's at the parade
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge naivety - well-intentioned but completely oblivious.

jokes.text: Cool! It's Lesbians of the Caribbean! Yo, ho, ho It's an alternative lifestyle for me
jokes.punchline: Clever play on words with lesbian theme
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels with good rhythm and pop culture integration.

jokes.text: Oh, a salute to brunch! Wave to brunch, Maggie.
jokes.punchline: Marge treating 'brunch' like it's a person to wave to
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge innocence treating abstract concepts as tangible things.

jokes.text: Oh, look at those abs. Everyone here has a six-pack and I'm the only one with a keg.
jokes.setup: Homer noticing fit people at the parade
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating beer belly comparison using alcohol terminology
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor with perfect alcohol metaphor that fits Homer's character perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, God! Cover up!
jokes.punchline: Marge's mortified reaction to Homer exposing himself
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassment humor that follows naturally from the setup.

jokes.text: Four tickets for Shenani-Goats!
jokes.punchline: Silly pun on 'shenanigans' with goats
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that's mildly amusing but not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: It may contain brief rudeness, adult explosions, and scenes with Garry Shandling.
jokes.punchline: Treating Garry Shandling as equivalent to violence and profanity
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of movie ratings with unexpected celebrity inclusion as a warning.

jokes.text: These are so easy. It's obviously Tom Hanks.
jokes.punchline: Wrong answer that shows his ignorance
jokes.explanation: Standard 'Homer is wrong' humor that's predictable but fits his character.

jokes.text: Otm Shank. He is India's answer to Brian Dennehy.
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up actor with specific cultural comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with perfect character voice and unexpected cultural reference comparison.

jokes.text: You suck! No wonder your parents are getting divorced!
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately harsh insult in what should be family content
jokes.explanation: Sharp contrast between expected wholesome content and brutal personal attack.

jokes.text: The professor said not to let him get a boner!
jokes.punchline: Double entendre using 'boner' (mistake vs. erection)
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre that works but isn't particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: I'm laughing! But it's a laugh of impatience! Show the movie!
jokes.punchline: Self-aware analysis of his own frustrated laughter
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with Homer being unusually self-aware about his emotional state.

jokes.text: So many previews. So many previews. So many previews.
jokes.punchline: Repetitive mantra showing his mental breakdown
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing Homer's simple mind getting stuck in a loop.

jokes.text: If that's a phrase you like to hear, then you'll love Movie Call!
jokes.setup: Audience expecting the movie to start
jokes.punchline: Another advertisement instead of the promised feature
jokes.explanation: Good bait-and-switch that plays on audience expectations about movie experiences.

jokes.text: Well, if we wanted to live forever, we wouldn't have become ushers.
jokes.setup: Chaos in the theater with angry crowd
jokes.punchline: Fatalistic acceptance of dangerous job conditions
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery with unexpected philosophical resignation from a minor character.

jokes.text: Hey, Dad, has the movie started yet?
jokes.setup: Homer already agitated about movie delays
jokes.punchline: Bart deliberately provoking him with obvious question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart mischief-making that shows his understanding of how to push Homer's buttons.

jokes.text: I'm not scared of those ushers. What are they gonna do? Advance on me?
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'advance' (approach vs. movie term) showing his obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels and shows Homer's unintentional wit.

jokes.text: Litter is my most treacherous foe. I would like to eat its children.
jokes.setup: Boxer giving speech about environmental responsibility
jokes.punchline: Applying violent boxing metaphors to litter cleanup
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor that perfectly captures the boxer's violent worldview applied to mundane topics.

jokes.text: My mouth. My beautiful mouth!
jokes.setup: Homer gets punched in the face
jokes.punchline: Lamenting the loss of his 'beautiful' mouth
jokes.explanation: Good irony since Homer's mouth is definitely not beautiful, plus typical Homer vanity.

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry. On a man his size, that just provides sexual release.
jokes.setup: Homer violently shaking from not being able to eat
jokes.punchline: Doctor's clinical assessment of Homer's physical response
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark humor that works through clinical detachment about inappropriate topic.

jokes.text: Hey, Dad, I'm gonna make a human yo-yo. If you object, clearly say no. No! No objections, eh? That's great.
jokes.setup: Homer can't speak clearly with wired jaw
jokes.punchline: Bart deliberately misinterpreting Homer's protests
jokes.explanation: Excellent exploitation of the episode's central premise with perfect Bart mischief.

jokes.text: Milhouse, you ready to imitate that Jackass show? All those disclaimers made me want to do it more!
jokes.punchline: Admitting disclaimers have opposite effect
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about how warnings can backfire, especially with kids.

jokes.text: Duffman can never die! Only the actors who play him!
jokes.setup: Someone says Duffman died of liver failure
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about corporate mascot vs. real people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on corporate mascots and the dark reality behind them. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: What beverage brewed since ancient times is made from hops and grains? How about ancient hop grain juice? Beer! Beer! Beer!
jokes.punchline: Barney's wrong answer followed by obvious correct answers
jokes.explanation: Standard bar trivia humor that fits the characters but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Ooh. Duff luck. I never would've figured that out. That's the kind of thing you just gotta know.
jokes.punchline: Barney treating basic knowledge as specialized information
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Barney's obliviousness despite being an alcoholic.

jokes.text: I am not gonna make you another sparerib smoothie. Most people with their jaws wired shut don't gain weight.
jokes.punchline: Homer somehow gaining weight despite inability to chew
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer joke - even medical restrictions can't stop his appetite and weight gain.

jokes.text: I was in the kitchen counting the corncobs on the curtains, when who should ring the doorbell but Ned Flanders.
jokes.setup: Marge describing her day to Homer
jokes.punchline: Revealing how mundane her activities are
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Marge's domestic life and attention to trivial details.

jokes.text: Pardon my French, but sometimes that man's a goofy-doofy.
jokes.punchline: The 'strong language' is completely innocent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge character comedy - her idea of profanity is adorable nonsense words.

jokes.text: Marge thinks Flanders is annoying. This marriage just got interesting.
jokes.punchline: Bart recognizing this as marital discord
jokes.explanation: Good insight into Bart's understanding of family dynamics and his mischievous appreciation of conflict.

jokes.text: We were playing foursquare and I called no double taps, and Ralph double taps. And I said, 'You're out,' and he says, 'I can do a summersault,' which had nothing to do with anything!
jokes.punchline: Ralph's completely irrelevant response to being called out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum characterization through Lisa's frustrated storytelling.

jokes.text: Maybe a hug will cork her cry hole.
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude but well-intentioned solution
jokes.explanation: Great Homer-ism - crude language paired with genuine parental concern.

jokes.text: So the substitute teacher comes in and says her name is Mrs. Doody, and everyone's looking at me like, 'Take it, Bart. Run with it.' Then it hits me. I've become a clown. A class clown. And it sickens me.
jokes.setup: Perfect setup for Bart's usual mischief
jokes.punchline: Bart having existential crisis about his role
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character development moment where Bart becomes self-aware about his comedic role.

jokes.text: Three wars back we called sauerkraut 'liberty cabbage,' and we called liberty cabbage 'super slaw!' And back then, a suitcase was known as a Swedish lunchbox. 'Course, nobody knew that but me.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd claims ending with admission of ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa rambling that starts with real history and devolves into nonsense.

jokes.text: Anyway, 'Long story short' is a phrase whose origins are complicated and rambling.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on the phrase itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor that's both self-referential and perfectly in character for rambling Grandpa.

jokes.text: That donkey is such a bad influence on you.
jokes.punchline: Marge blaming donkey for Homer's behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback that works as character comedy for Marge's tendency to blame external influences.

jokes.text: A formal! The one place you can wear a tiara and not look crazy.
jokes.setup: Marge getting excited about formal event
jokes.punchline: Revealing her secret desire to wear a tiara
jokes.explanation: Great character insight into Marge's hidden desires and social awareness.

jokes.text: That's quite an ear. Have some money.
jokes.punchline: Random monetary reward for trivial knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about wealthy people's arbitrary generosity.

jokes.text: Your husband is quite the gentleman. Most people laugh at my googly eye.
jokes.punchline: Revealing she has a googly eye that people usually mock
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that explains Homer's unusually polite behavior.

jokes.text: Well, I've never seen a man politely pass up so many hors d'oeuvres. My husband, Maxtone Witherball, has already had three shrimp!
jokes.punchline: Contrast with husband's gluttony and ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Good contrast humor with nice absurd name for the wealthy character.

jokes.text: Someone's done her henpecking.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of domineering behavior
jokes.explanation: Decent self-aware humor about marital dynamics.

jokes.text: I'm horny.
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt sexual admission with jaw wired shut
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer bluntness that creates cringe humor in romantic setting.

jokes.text: I don't know what you said, but I'm sure it was beautiful.
jokes.punchline: Marge's sweet misinterpretation of crude remark
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge sweetness creating ironic humor through misunderstanding.

jokes.text: And Reverend, I'll put in those pec implants on Thursday.
jokes.punchline: Religious figure getting cosmetic surgery
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about religious hypocrisy and vanity.

jokes.text: I'll have to take off my shoes to cover the odor.
jokes.punchline: Marge's shoes smell worse than Homer's breath
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about competing bad odors.

jokes.text: Hello? Yeah. I'd like to arrange for an escort, please. To where? How about Orgasmville! You... Hello? Hello?
jokes.setup: Homer making what seems like escort call
jokes.punchline: Revealing he thinks escort means transportation service
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that plays on Homer's innocence and sexual crudeness simultaneously.

jokes.text: I'm an alcoholic.
jokes.setup: TV producer casually mentioning why he's at the bar
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of serious problem
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery about serious topic treated casually.

jokes.text: My boyfriend has a metal tongue stud. Who cares what's on his tongue, as long as he's a stud where it counts! I'm talking downtown!
jokes.setup: Question about kissing someone with metal in mouth
jokes.punchline: Audience member's inappropriate sexual oversharing
jokes.explanation: Standard daytime TV audience oversharing humor.

jokes.text: We'd be making love and he'd have a mouth full of Hershey's Miniatures. Krackel was my favorite.
jokes.punchline: Oversharing intimate details on TV
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Marge innocently oversharing on television.

jokes.text: I hope that was the only miniature in the bedroom. I'm talking downtown!
jokes.punchline: Host making crude joke about Homer's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual humor typical of daytime TV parody.

jokes.text: Ostrich burgers! Get your ostrich burgers! I'll take one. Okay. You pick one out and I'll punch it to death.
jokes.punchline: Offering to kill the ostrich fresh on demand
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with perfect escalation and dark comedy elements.

jokes.text: Dude, I thought we were friends!
jokes.punchline: Ostrich expressing betrayal in human terms
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist follow-up that humanizes the animal in unexpected way.

jokes.text: Well, okay. For you, I'll be as dull as Dilbert.
jokes.punchline: Using Dilbert as the epitome of dullness
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that effectively conveys Homer's promise of boredom.

jokes.text: We're rehearsing a play. Yes, and I was merely reading the title, I'm Gonna Kill You.
jokes.setup: Homer threatens neighbor, then covers when caught
jokes.punchline: Absurd excuse about play title
jokes.explanation: Good quick thinking humor with absurd but believable excuse.

jokes.text: I just poured myself a new glass of milk. The old one sat out for a little while.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his boring new life
jokes.punchline: Treating minor milk replacement as noteworthy event
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing how mundane Homer's life has become.

jokes.text: Good, good, good, good, good, good, good!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince himself everything is fine
jokes.punchline: Repetitive mantra revealing his desperation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's mental state deteriorating through repetition.

jokes.text: Vendors, start your gouging!
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging vendor price exploitation
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about sporting event economics.

jokes.text: Catch you later, radiator!
jokes.punchline: Punning farewell after car damage
jokes.explanation: Good pun that shows Marge getting into the spirit of destruction.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I hit someone. Then I taunted him. I've never felt more alive!
jokes.punchline: Sudden realization she loves violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent character reversal showing Marge discovering her dark side with perfect escalation.

jokes.text: Don't hurt me! I'm not like you people! I'm loved!
jokes.setup: Marge in danger at demolition derby
jokes.punchline: Claiming superiority while begging for mercy
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Marge's class consciousness even in danger.

jokes.text: I caught a head! Oh, dang! It's been scooped out.
jokes.punchline: Disappointment that severed head is empty
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with unexpected disappointment about gruesome discovery.

jokes.text: This beer's deliskous. I'm gonna saves me wife.
jokes.punchline: Slurred speech showing immediate intoxication
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer drinking humor with immediate effects shown through speech.

jokes.text: A man on a donkey! Never in my six weeks on the job have I seen the likes of this.
jokes.punchline: Announcer's inexperience making this seem unprecedented
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with announcer's rookie status adding to the comedy.

jokes.text: Looks like I needs some fuel for me mule. Gas for me ass.
jokes.setup: Homer on donkey needing to refuel
jokes.punchline: Crude rhyming pun about donkey fuel
jokes.explanation: Good crude pun that fits drunk Homer's reduced mental state.

jokes.text: That's okay, Marge. You're a good wet blanket. The kind I like wrapped around me.
jokes.punchline: Homer turning criticism into romantic metaphor
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment turning insult into expression of love with perfect Homer logic.

jokes.text: When Homer's jaw was crushed, crushed, crushed, his life took a nitro-burning funny turn! Oh, yeah! It's important to grow, grow, grow, but if you take it to the max, max, max, it's gonna get ugly! In the mud!
jokes.setup: Episode recap in monster truck style
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top repetitive narration
jokes.explanation: Good parody of monster truck/extreme sports narration style but feels somewhat forced.

tmdb_overview: A jaw injury from colliding with a new town statue turns Homer into a better listener while recuperating with his jaws wired shut, but once the wires come off, Homer does not go back to being loud and obnoxious and Marge becomes starved for thrills.
